steps.title: Identify structures on each side of the blank
steps.content:
Left side: “while” introduces a dependent clause.
Right side: “is” is the main verb of the sentence.

steps.title: Apply the boundaries rule for subject and main verb
steps.content: A noun phrase subject must directly precede its main verb without punctuation inserted between them. The subject is “the reason seeds... while falling,” which must connect directly to the main verb “is.” All three incorrect choices insert punctuation that breaks this connection. That matches choice D — D is correct.
